content:
4 min read
Just now

--

I still remember the Slack message.
“Who deleted all the comments?”
It was followed by a screenshot showing nearly every green line disappearing from the pull request.
Over 10,000 lines.
At first glance it looked like the kind of commit that gets you fired.
By the end of the review, our principal engineer wrote just one sentence.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

“This is one of the best cleanup PRs I’ve seen.”
That wasn’t because I had removed comments.
It was because I had removed lies.
The Service That Had More Comments Than Logic
The service had been around for years.
It started as a small C++ backend, then parts of it slowly moved to Go as different teams modernized sections. After enough migrations, enough deadlines, and enough people touching it, the codebase became something nobody fully understood.
Everyone had the same habit.
Whenever something looked confusing, they added another comment.
